findings.problem: Several key beats slip out of Suna's grounded, domestic register into authorial abstraction and future-pointing summary. That weakens close-third fidelity and blunts the flat, matter-of-fact strangeness the style bible calls for.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the interpretive sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:75, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:111, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:127, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:143 so the scene stays inside what Suna can concretely notice, compare, or fear in the moment. Replace the future-looking summary at line 75 and the abstract explanatory phrasing in lines 111, 127, and 143 with sensory residue, practical inference, or a shorter Suna-native thought that lets the uncanny remain unresolved on the page.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:75, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:111, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:127, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:143 read as immediate Suna perception rather than narratorial essaying: no future-looking forecast at line 75, no abstract summary of how grief has been shaped at line 111, and no speculative bodily or hereditary explanation at line 143. FAIL if those lines still tell the reader what the moment means instead of letting Suna encounter it.

findings.problem: The chapter reuses the same correction formula at multiple high-pressure beats (It was not X. It was Y.). By the third use, the syntax feels mechanical and pre-interpreted, flattening moments that should land through action, sound, or silence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the sentence shapes around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:97, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117 so the beats do not all arrive through the same not-X/actually-Y scaffold. Keep the meaning, but vary the delivery: let at least two of these moments register through gesture, timing, or a character's physical response instead of an explicit narratorial correction.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after revision, no more than one of the target spans at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:49, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:97, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:117 still uses the exact It was not correction pattern, and the other beats remain fully legible by reading the passage. FAIL if all three moments still explain themselves with the same sentence construction.

summary: Chapter 4 cleanly lands the canonical uncanny revelation, preserves continuity, and keeps the spoken voices natural and distinct. It fails award and prose standards because the narration repeatedly steps outside Suna's voice to interpret the scene and leans on a visible sentence formula at pivotal beats, which keeps strong material from landing with full shortlist-level force.
